task.title: Enabling remote synchronization
task.shortdesc: Remote synchronization is available from the Navigator in the Resource perspective or the view in the perspective. To connect Enterprise Service Tools single-service projects to a remote system, open the Navigator and look for your projects there. The EST Project Explorer does not support remote synchronization.

keywords.indexterm: connecting a local project to a remote system

taskbody.prereq:
The remote synchronization function is deprecated.Before you can use remote synchronization, you must complete the following tasks:
Define a local project and its resources.
Create a connection to a remote system.
Connect to the remote system. For more information, see the related links.

steps.stepsection: To enable remote synchronization of a project (that is, to connect a local project to a remote system), do these steps:

step.cmd: Open the Navigator, view, or Data Project Explorer.
Select the project that you want to connect to a remote system and then click Remote Enable Remote Synchronization from the pop–up menu.
step.stepresult: The Remote System Settings window opens

step.cmd: From the Host Name list, select a connected remote system.
From the High-level Qualifier list, select a high-level qualifier for your remote data sets.
Select a property group to associate with the remote resources.
step.info: The property group defines settings for building and running an application on the remote system.If a property group is associated with the remote resources, you can skip this step. To create a new property group, click New. For information about property groups, see the related links.

step.cmd: To define local-to-remote mappings for your project, click Enable Managed Synchronization?.

info.note: If you want to skip this step, you can add mappings later in the Pushable Manifest Editor. For more information, see .

step.cmd: Use the mappings table to associate local file extensions to remote locations as follows:

substep.cmd: To add a mapping, click Add. Type a local file extension in the Extension field and a remote data set name in the MVS Mapping field and click OK.
To remove a mapping, select it from the table and click Remove.
To edit a mapping, select it in the table and click Edit.

step.cmd: Click Finish to save your changes.

taskbody.result:
This process enables remote synchronization by creating the following files in the project: .RemoteMapping This file stores local-to-remote file mappings for the project. To add, remove, or edit mappings, double-click this file. The Pushable Manifest Editor opens.
.RemoteSyntaxProperties This file stores remote system properties for the project. To edit these properties, select the project name and then select Remote Properties from the menu. Do not edit this file manually; use the properties page to edit it instead. Multiple users can share the properties by keeping the .RemoteSyntaxProperties file in a central repository. For local z/OS projects, this process also enables remote syntax check. Because remote syntax check requires information about the remote system to set up default properties, you must be connected to a remote system before you enable remote synchronization on a local z/OS project. Local z/OS project refers to workstation-based projects, such as local COBOL or PL/I projects. Do not confuse workstation-based projects with remote z/OS projects and subprojects.
